description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for PIM-SM?
Feature Caveat: PIM-SM - Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for CMF?
Feature Caveat: CMF - No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

feature: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping
description: DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping?

Feature Caveat: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping - DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

description: Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

feature: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces
description: IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces?
Feature Caveat: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces - IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

description: Multicast traffic cannot be run under GRE, 6in4, or 6in6 tunnels.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Multicast?
Feature Caveat: Multicast - Multicast traffic cannot be run under GRE, 6in4, or 6in6 tunnels.

description: * VXLAN must be configured prior to configuring VSX. IPv6 multicast is not supported for VXLAN overlay. Multicast support for static VXLAN in the overlay has limited support. Contact Aruba Support for details.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Multicast and VXLAN?

Feature Caveat: Multicast and VXLAN - VXLAN must be configured prior to configuring VSX. IPv6 multicast is not supported for VXLAN overlay. Multicast support for static VXLAN in the overlay has limited support. Contact Aruba Support for details.

description: Priority-based flow control (PFC) is not supported on a split port.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for PFC?
Feature Caveat: PFC - Priority-based flow control (PFC) is not supported on a split port.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: Issuing the traceroute command with the ip-option loosesourceroute parameter fails inan overlay EVPN-VxLAN deployment.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Traceroute?
Feature Caveat: Traceroute - Issuing the traceroute command with the ip-option loosesourceroute parameter fails inan overlay EVPN-VxLAN deployment.

description: Traceroute v4/v6 over VXLAN fails to find intermediate next-hop IP information from a source VTEP in Virtual Active Gateway environment (the SVI is the same as theActive Gateway IP).

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what limitation is mentioned for Traceroute?

Feature Caveat: Traceroute - Traceroute v4/v6 over VXLAN fails to find intermediate next-hop IP information from a source VTEP in Virtual Active Gateway environment (the SVI is the same as theActive Gateway IP).

description: Symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured. Scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log. Workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.
symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured.
scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log.
workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.

Workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.

description: Symptom: IP packets containing options headers bypass routed-in security policies configured with drop actions, resulting in policy enforcement failure. Scenario: When security policies are applied in the routed-in direction with drop actions configured, IP packets containing options headers are being processed by the policy engine but are circumventing the intended policy enforcement, causing these packets to be incorrectly forwarded through the system instead of being dropped as intended.

description: Symptom: Error when configuring an username for cloud-managed feature packs if the length of said username is greater than 32 characters. Scenario: When configuring the feature pack server information on a CX switch, configure an username with a length greater than 32 characters. Workaround: Create an username to obtain cloud-managed feature packs from ASP with a length less than 32 characters.
symptom: Error when configuring an username for cloud-managed feature packs if the length of said username is greater than 32 characters.

description: Symptom: Switches may encounter service disruptions or become unresponsive, leading to connectivity loss or requiring reconnection to Central. Scenario: Certain operations, such as dry runs or Central-related activities, can trigger failures or cause system hangs in critical services like restd, smfd and yang-resolverd. These issues can render the system unresponsive, preventing dependent services from re-establishing connectivity.

scenario: Certain operations, such as dry runs or Central-related activities, can trigger failures or cause system hangs in critical services like restd, smfd and yang-resolverd. These issues can render the system unresponsive, preventing dependent services from re-establishing connectivity.

description: Symptom: An STP port incorrectly transitions into Disabled/Blocked state. Scenario: During certain port flap events, STP may erroneously mark a port as Disabled/Blocked, even when the link should remain active. Workaround: Shut down all affected ports. Restart the hpe-pvstd process using the following commands: start-shell sudo bash ps aux | grep hpe-pvstd | grep -v grep systemctl restart hpe-pvstd ps aux | grep hpe-pvstd | grep -v grep exit exit
symptom: An STP port incorrectly transitions into Disabled/Blocked state.
scenario: During certain port flap events, STP may erroneously mark a port as Disabled/Blocked, even when the link should remain active.
workaround: Shut down all affected ports. Restart the hpe-pvstd process using the following commands: start-shell sudo bash ps aux | grep hpe-pvstd | grep -v grep systemctl restart hpe-pvstd ps aux | grep hpe-pvstd | grep -v grep exit exit

PVST (Bug ID 389395): The symptom is: An STP port incorrectly transitions into Disabled/Blocked state.

description: Symptom: The VSX daemon may become unresponsive or hang due to a deadlock condition. Scenario: This issue can occur under rare circumstances when a device with an active peer connection loses that connection and another thread simultaneously attempts to access the peer connection. Workaround: Restart the VSX daemon.
symptom: The VSX daemon may become unresponsive or hang due to a deadlock condition.

description: Symptom: The first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped. Scenario: This issue can occur when no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and client MAC is not learnt yet. Workaround: Subsequent DHCP client retry is successful.

description: Symptom: Notification clients stop receiving updates, sessions drop, a core dump is recorded, and the REST service restarts. Scenario: This issue may occur when a subscription is added or removed at the same exact time notifications are being delivered. Workaround: Wait for the REST service to complete its automatic restart, then reconnect the notification clients and reapply the desired subscriptions.

description: Symptom: The port connected to the ESXi server shows as up with a disabled role and a blocking state, causing traffic interruptions. Scenario: This issue occurs in a VSX configuration where specific ports are connected to an ESXi server. After the configuration change, the spanning tree does not recognize the port correctly, resulting in it being blocked.
symptom: The port connected to the ESXi server shows as up with a disabled role and a blocking state, causing traffic interruptions.
scenario: This issue occurs in a VSX configuration where specific ports are connected to an ESXi server. After the configuration change, the spanning tree does not recognize the port correctly, resulting in it being blocked.

description: Symptom: Internal server error encountered when attempting to access 8360 switches through the Central management interface. Scenario: This issue arises when attempting to onboard two 8360 switches that are connected to Central. Clicking on the switch names in the Central UI triggers a backend API call that fails due to an invalid firmware version returned from the switches.
symptom: Internal server error encountered when attempting to access 8360 switches through the Central management interface.
scenario: This issue arises when attempting to onboard two 8360 switches that are connected to Central. Clicking on the switch names in the Central UI triggers a backend API call that fails due to an invalid firmware version returned from the switches.
